skill.name: database-optimization
skill.description: SQL query optimization and database performance guidance for slow queries, indexes, N+1 problems, caching, migrations, and monitoring.
skill.summary: Improve database speed with query analysis, indexing guidance, N+1 fixes, caching patterns, and performance monitoring advice.
skill.author: Joseph OBrien

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found Markdown code fences and database examples only. No executable scripts, network calls, credential access, prompt injection attempts, or malicious behavior were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command alerts are fenced Markdown examples for SQL, Python, Ruby, and user prompts. They are not runnable skill code and do not execute shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched content is inside Markdown fences or example output. There is no script file, command runner, or instruction that would execute these snippets automatically.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Database Terminology
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts match ordinary words and database guidance, such as description text, index examples, and includes examples. No cryptographic algorithm use is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed lines contain database optimization prose and ORM examples. I found no MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptography implementation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Pattern in SQL Examples
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts are database examples such as SELECT * and monitoring advice. They do not inspect the host system or collect local environment data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines discuss SQL query shape and index maintenance. No host reconnaissance commands, filesystem probing, or environment enumeration appear in the reviewed files.

content.value_statement: Slow queries and missing indexes can make applications feel unreliable. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code analyze database performance and propose practical fixes.
actual_capabilities: Reviews SQL queries for common performance problems.
Suggests indexes based on filters, joins, ordering, and selected columns.
Explains N+1 query patterns and eager loading options.
Recommends caching strategies for expensive read paths.
Outlines migration and monitoring practices for performance work.
limitations: It cannot measure production performance without real query plans or metrics.
It does not connect to databases or run SQL by itself.
Index suggestions must be tested against real data volume and write load.
Caching advice requires application-specific invalidation rules.

use_cases.title: Tune Slow Queries
use_cases.description: Review a slow SQL query, identify likely bottlenecks, and propose clearer query and index changes.

use_cases.title: Resolve ORM N+1 Problems
use_cases.description: Find repeated query patterns in application code and suggest eager loading or prefetch strategies.

use_cases.title: Plan Performance Improvements
use_cases.description: Create a practical optimization plan for indexes, caching, migrations, and monitoring.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Slow Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this SQL query for performance problems. Explain likely bottlenecks and suggest safe improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Suggest Indexes
prompt_templates.prompt: Given this query and table description, recommend indexes. Explain column order, tradeoffs, and validation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Fix N+1 Queries
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this ORM code for N+1 query behavior. Suggest eager loading, batching, or query changes.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Optimization Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a database optimization plan using these slow query logs, schema details, and traffic patterns.

output_examples.input: A users lookup by email takes 450 ms on a large table.
output_examples.output: The output explains the likely full table scan, suggests an email index, recommends selecting only needed columns, and lists validation checks.

output_examples.input: A page loads users and then queries posts for each user.
output_examples.output: The output identifies an N+1 pattern, suggests eager loading posts, and notes memory and pagination tradeoffs.

output_examples.input: A dashboard repeatedly loads expensive aggregate queries.
output_examples.output: The output recommends measuring query cost, considering materialized summaries or cache TTLs, and defining invalidation rules.

best_practices: Share query plans, table sizes, indexes, and slow query timings when asking for optimization help.
Validate every index recommendation with production-like data before deployment.
Measure before and after each change to avoid hidden regressions.
anti_patterns: Do not add indexes to every column without checking read and write tradeoffs.
Do not cache data without a clear invalidation strategy.
Do not optimize isolated queries without considering application access patterns.

faq.question: Does this skill run database commands?
faq.answer: No. It provides analysis and recommendations based on information you share.

faq.question: Which databases does it support?
faq.answer: It focuses on PostgreSQL and MySQL patterns, with general guidance for other relational databases.

faq.question: Can it replace a database administrator?
faq.answer: No. It helps prepare recommendations, but high-risk changes still need expert review and testing.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the query, schema, indexes, row counts, execution plan, and observed latency.

faq.question: Can it help with caching?
faq.answer: Yes. It can suggest cache keys, TTL choices, and invalidation approaches for expensive reads.

faq.question: Is it safe for production systems?
faq.answer: Use it for guidance only. Test all database changes in staging before production deployment.
